title: Sleep Tonic Shelf
desc: The patrol rest niche is down to its last proper dose of dreamless tonic, and the record shelf looks worse than the tired guards do.

steps.text:
No one posted to Fear Valley expects ordinary sleep, only manageable sleep. In the patrol rest niche near the upper return path, a small locked shelf holds dreamless tonic, record slates, and the rotation notes that decide who goes back into the rift after a few hours' rest and who spends the shift hauling themselves together with professional contempt. Tonight the tonic jars are nearly gone, and the slate shelf has fallen into the usual post-rotation confusion.

The niche clerk holds up two stoppered bottles against the lamp. "Mix a fresh tonic batch if you want the bench work," she says. "Or sort the slate shelf and dose ledger if you prefer records. Both matter. Fear Valley punishes tired mistakes with unusual creativity."

options.label: Mix a fresh dreamless tonic batch at the bench
Sort the slate shelf and dose ledger

steps.text:
The tonic is less mystical than its reputation suggests and more exacting in its proportions. Bitter root reduction, cooling mineral water, a scraping of black salt, and one narrow measure of the clerk's herb concentrate stirred in until the liquid loses its cloud and turns uniformly dark. You mix carefully, bottle the finished batch, and label each jar with the night's date because no one in this niche trusts an unlabeled sleeping draught under rift conditions.

The clerk uncaps one bottle, checks the scent, and seems reassured by how unpleasant it is. "Good. If it tastes like regret and chalk, it usually works." In UnderWorld medicine, that appears to be a standard worth preserving.

options.label: Stopper the last bottle and leave the shelf supplied

steps.text:
The slate shelf holds rotation notes, dose counts, and short ugly summaries of who reported what after each patrol. Under normal circumstances it would be a simple records job. In Fear Valley, that means every slate also contains traces of someone's doubt about whether what they saw belonged in writing at all. You sort the slates by date and route, return the dose ledger to the front slot, and stack the fresh report tablets where the next clerk can actually reach them before another tired patrol wanders in.

Once the shelf is back in order, the niche looks less like a confession room and more like a functional outpost again. The clerk glances over the restored rows and nods once. "Better. If nightmares are going to remain common, the bookkeeping should at least stay competent."

options.label: Set the ledger straight and leave the niche organized
